steps.text: Run unspiked blank samples each time a sampling procedure is completed.
Acquire duplicate or replicate samples whenever feasible.
Run spiked samples or positive controls for each sampling procedure to establish that the technique was performed correctly.
Run trip or travel blanks to document contamination attributable to shipping and field handling procedures.

preconditions.text: The corresponding health and safety plan must be approved by the local Safety, Health and Environmental Management (SHEM) office or the chemical hygiene officer before starting a project.
A management system should be in place that accurately reflects before sampling or analytical work begins.

constraints.text: Safety is a prime consideration in any sampling event.
Personal protective equipment and first aid should be addressed in the sampling technique and validation plan.
Additional safety concerns include physical and biological hazards such as harmful microorganisms.
